title: Nir Barzilai on Centenarian Genetics, GLP-1 as Longevity Drug
description: Gerontologist Nir Barzilai discusses centenarian genetics revealing 80-90% heritability, and emerging evidence that low-dose GLP-1 drugs extend lifespan 7-10% in mice independent of weight loss.

title: Mitochondria and Longevity: Hype vs. Science
description: Experts weigh in on the mitochondria wellness trend — exercise does stimulate mitochondrial biogenesis, but NAD+ supplement claims outpace the clinical evidence.

title: NAD+ Anti-Aging Supplements: Don't Waste Your Money
description: Chief Longevity Officer at London Regenerative Institute warns NR and NMN supplements show limited clinical benefit despite marketing hype around NAD+ replenishment.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026
description: Monthly roundup covering FDA-cleared cellular reprogramming trial by Life Biosciences, senolytic effectiveness via mitochondrial stress, and CAR-T cells targeting Alzheimer's amyloid plaques.

title: Juvenescence Completes Phase 1 Trial for First-in-Class PAI-1 Inhibitor
description: Juvenescence advances its PAI-1 inhibitor through Phase 1, moving closer to tackling key aging mechanisms and fibrotic disease pathways.

title: Swiss Study: Centenarian Blood Proteins Resemble Those of Younger Adults
description: First-of-its-kind Swiss proteomics study finds centenarians carry blood protein signatures resembling much younger individuals, offering new biomarker insights.

title: New Oral Diabetes Pill Shows Strong Blood Sugar Control and Weight Loss
description: A new oral GLP-1 pill demonstrates stronger blood sugar control and weight loss than existing options, signaling a shift toward patient-friendly metabolic treatments.

title: Morrow: Moving Longevity Beyond Elite Clinics with AI-Powered Care
description: Morrow's founders explain how lifestyle medicine combined with AI could democratize longevity care beyond wealthy clinics.

title: OrganTech Discovers New Cell Type Needed to Regenerate Hair Follicles
description: Researchers identify a previously undiscovered cell type essential for hair follicle regeneration in vivo, advancing regenerative medicine.

title: $30.8M ARPA-H Award Funds Cambrian Bio's Preventive Aging Drug
description: ARPA-H awards Cambrian Bio $30.8M through PROSPR program to develop selective mTORC1 inhibitors aimed at preserving intrinsic capacity before age-related decline.

title: $22M ARPA-H Contract to Test Anti-Retrotransposon Drug for Aging
description: Multi-institutional consortium receives $22M ARPA-H contract to test TPN-101, an antiviral drug that suppresses retrotransposon-driven inflammation linked to aging.

title: Stanford THRIVE Coalition Secures $34.5M from ARPA-H
description: Stanford-led THRIVE coalition wins up to $34.5M from ARPA-H to build an FDA-grade intrinsic capacity score predicting 20-year health outcomes.

title: Why Longevity Might Be Biopharma's Next Big Thing
description: Longevity startups attracted $8.5B in VC in 2024. Novartis struck a $550M deal with BioAge Labs, while ARPA-H's PROSPR program targets extending healthspan by 20 years.

title: Single-Cell Chromatin Map Reveals Sex-Specific Aging Across 21 Tissues
description: New single-cell chromatin atlas across 21 tissues reveals coordinated, sex-specific epigenetic remodeling with age, hinting at systemic aging drivers.

title: Blood Immune Signal Could Spot Alzheimer's Earlier
description: A common immune signal in routine blood tests could enable earlier Alzheimer's detection, linking diagnosis with genetics and longer brain health.

title: Rapamycin: The Dimmer Switch Dilemma — Evidence Review
description: Comprehensive review finds rapamycin shows promising preclinical anti-aging signals but zero qualifying human trials directly demonstrate it slows biological aging.

title: Life Biosciences Secures First FDA-Cleared Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences, co-founded by David Sinclair, receives FDA approval for Phase 1 trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming to restore vision in glaucoma and NAION patients.

title: Klothea Launches Phase 1b Human Trial of Alpha-Klotho mRNA Therapy
description: Klothea begins first human trial of AKL003 mRNA therapy to boost alpha-Klotho protein levels, with lifespan as the primary endpoint rather than disease.

title: FDA Ends 'Two Trial Dogma' — One Pivotal Trial May Now Suffice
description: The FDA officially allows a single well-designed pivotal trial for drug approval, potentially shaving 3-5 years off development timelines for aging therapeutics.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Lifting Weights Makes Your Brain Look Younger
description: One year of strength training reduced brain age by 1.4-2.3 years and enhanced functional connectivity, with effects persisting at two-year follow-up.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Heat Exposure as Antidepressant — Sauna Research
description: Deliberate heat exposure produced antidepressant effects lasting 6-8 weeks in major depressive disorder patients; combined with CBT showed ~16-point improvement.

title: ER-Phagy Identified as Novel Longevity Pathway and Drug Target
description: Researchers discover ER-phagy as a conserved mechanism linking endoplasmic reticulum turnover to aging and lifespan, presenting a new druggable pathway.

title: Longevity Economy Projected to Reach $63B by 2035
description: Biohacking goes mainstream in 2026 as the longevity economy is projected to nearly triple from 2024 levels, driven by consumer access to personalized genetic testing.

title: Longevity Biotech Stocks Surge as $27T Healthcare Shift Favors Cell Restoration
description: Global capital rotating into longevity infrastructure valued at $27T by 2030, with cell therapy markets hitting $8.85B and AI-driven drug discovery at $24.51B.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Whole Food Protein Over 'Protein Maxxing' Trend
description: Dr. Patrick warns that ultra-processed 'high protein' products are still processed food — prioritize whole food sources like meat, fish, eggs, and legumes.
